---
artifact_id: ESE111-CH04-GUIDE
pair_id: ESE111-CH04-GUIDE
lang: en
pair_revision: 0.1.0
semantic_id: ESE111-CH04-GUIDE
id: ESE111-CH04-GUIDE-en
resource_type: course-syllabus
language: en
paired_with: ESE111-CH04-GUIDE-fr
version: 0.1.0
lifecycle: drafting
outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
prerequisites: []
estimated_student_time_min: 635
safety_level: L1
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
technical_contract: &contract
  outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
  equations: [KCL signed current sum equals zero, KVL signed loop voltage sum equals zero, v equals R times i, power equals v times i]
  values: {source_v: 6.0, r1_ohm: 10000, r2_ohm: 22000, r3_ohm: 33000, r4_baseline_ohm: 15000, r4_redesign_ohm: 22000}
  safety_limits: {source_max_v: 6.1, current_limit_max_a: 0.02, dmm_current_input_permitted: false}
  expected_ranges: {baseline_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.087, 1.155], redesign_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.412, 1.500], source_current_simulated_a: [0.000229, 0.000244]}
  rubric_total: 28
  answer_key_digest: pending-restricted-store
  figure_ids: [ESE111-CH04-FIG-001]
---

# Chapter 4 unit guide — Kirchhoff models

This vertical unit turns a schematic into a prediction, a physical test, a fault
diagnosis, and a verified design change. It is a draft authoring baseline, not an
authorized activity: the deploying institution must approve the apparatus,
supervision, and safety record before learners energize it.

## What you will be able to do

By the end, you will assign references, solve the two-node network, make safe
voltage measurements, quantify disagreement, localize an open or wrong-value
branch, and redesign R4 so TP-B is between 1.40 V and 1.52 V while source current
stays at or below 0.25 mA. A correct number alone is insufficient; the required
evidence is a reproducible chain from assumption to acceptance test.

## Sequence and workload

| Resource | Suggested order | Typical time | Evidence |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Prerequisite diagnostic | 1 | 25 min | targeted remediation choice |
| Chapter and retrieval | 2 | 150 min | prediction and worked model |
| Interactive studio/demo | 3 | 90 min | reasoning checkpoints and exit ticket |
| Problem set | 4 | 120 min | individual calculations and design trade study |
| Guided laboratory | 5 | 150 min | inspected setup, raw readings, fault log, redesign result |
| Practical check | 6 | 25 min | individual model–measure–debug defense |
| Reflection/remediation | 7 | 75 min | corrected evidence or enrichment extension |

## Evidence, feedback, and mastery

The common 28-point rubric allocates four points each to requirements, modeling,
implementation, measurement, debugging, communication, and responsibility.
Mastery requires at least 20 points, Level 3 performance in model, measurement,
and debugging, and no safety or integrity failure. Feedback follows the
diagnostic, studio exit ticket, problem set, and lab checkpoint. Reassessment
uses changed values and a different reversible fault.

Students may work in English or French at the same technical threshold. The
schematic, parameters, points, safety rules, and allowed resources are identical.
Technical meaning and evidence are assessed, not accent or nonessential grammar.

## Safety and access

Use only an approved protected source, no more than 6.1 V, configured to no more
than 20 mA. Keep the output off while changing the circuit. The DMM leads stay in
COM and V/Ω; current-input use is outside this unit. Stop on damage, unstable or
unexpected values, current-limit indication, heat, odor, or any uncertainty about
the configuration. Before G1, the instructor controls every energization.

Equivalent access may provide manipulation assistance when dexterity is not the
construct, but the learner still directs the wiring, selects settings/test points,
interprets raw evidence, and defends the diagnosis. Print, keyboard, offline,
high-contrast SVG, and tabular routes carry the same decisions.

## Required materials and offline route

The minimal route uses the approved source, one function-checked DMM with known
input resistance, a breadboard, 1% resistors, and leads. The standard route adds a
bench supply and a second voltage channel. Downloaded sources, the editable SVG,
the deterministic Python model, and static data fallback require no internet.
Prepared or simulated data supports analysis only; it cannot certify personal
construction, instrument use, or physical debugging.

## Current limitations

The context candidate, physical apparatus paths, second-instructor rehearsal,
learner pilots, and independent EN/FR reviews are still pending. Until those gates
close, use this package only for controlled review and authorized formative work.
